results.title: TMRW Closes $7m Seed Round with Tidal Ventures, Partners with TruDiagnostic | Mark Britt posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
View profile for Mark Britt

TMRW•19K followers

We’ve officially closed our $7m seed round led by Tidal Ventures and inked an exclusive Australian partnership with TruDiagnostic to bring world-leading epigenetic testing to our members.
It was great to chat with Bronwen Clune from Capital Brief recently about why TMRW is taking a different path than most startups. I believe the "building in public" trend is often just an entrepreneur’s ego vision; instead, as we scale, we are committed to building with our patients.
In health, our focus must be on prioritising and optimising them rather than just hitting a "clinical minimum". By using data to increase human connection, we are ensuring our members are truly in charge of their own health journey.

View organization page for Capital Brief [...] The round was led by Sydney-based Tidal Ventures, with Melbourne fund Capital Zed and a cohort of high-net-worth entrepreneurs and family offices also participating — many of whom are already TMRW members.
Alongside the funding, TMRW has also secured an exclusive Australian partnership with TruDiagnostic, the US-based leader in epigenetic and biological age testing, whose technology is used by longevity advocates Bryan Johnson and Kim Kardashian. [...] Alongside the raise, TMRW is announcing an exclusive Australian partnership with TruDiagnostic, the US-based global leader in epigenetic and biological age testing, already used by longevity fans Bryan Johnson and Kim Kardashian.
Read the full story by Bronwen Clune 👉

results.title: TMRW lands $7m to expand longevity clinics
results.content:
“We’ve officially closed our $7m seed round led by Tidal Ventures and inked an exclusive Australian partnership with TruDiagnostic to bring world-leading epigenetic testing to our members,” Britt wrote in a recent post on LinkedIn.

Britt mentioned that it was a pleasure to speak with Bronwen Clune from Capital Brief regarding TMRW’s unique trajectory compared to other startups. She expressed her belief that the “building in public” trend is frequently driven by an entrepreneur’s ego; she noted that, instead, TMRW is committed to building alongside its patients as the company scales.

It’s a broader trend across the longevity field, in which we’re moving from passive tracking to active intervention.

#### A partnership built around biological age [...] # TMRW lands $7m to expand longevity clinics

## Longevity.Technology is growing fast; we are open to conversations with prospective partners and investors who share our vision.

## Please get in touch if you are interested:

## TMRW secures seed funding and partners with TruDiagnostic to bring deeper biological age testing to Australia’s growing longevity market.

Are we measuring age wrong? Your cells may already have the answer. That idea is quickly becoming one of the driving forces behind the longevity economy. As consumers grow more curious about how quickly their bodies are aging, a wave of startups has emerged, promising insights through blood testing and digital health dashboards. [...] #### A partnership built around biological age

Alongside the funding announcement, TMRW also revealed an exclusive Australian partnership with US-based diagnostics company TruDiagnostic.

The company specializes in epigenetic testing, a type of analysis that examines how lifestyle, environment and behavior influence the way genes function over time. This testing can estimate a person’s “biological age,” a measure of how quickly their body is aging compared with their chronological age.

The technology has already attracted high-profile users in the longevity world, including entrepreneur Bryan Johnson and celebrity Kim Kardashian.

# TMRW Raises $4.97M Seed Round Led by Tidal Ventures

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

Startup Researcher Oceania

674 followers

 Report this post

Longevity platform TMRW has raised a US$4.97 million seed round led by Tidal Ventures, with participation from Blackbird Ventures, Archangel Ventures, and Possible Ventures. Founded by Mark Britt, the company provides personalised longevity programs based on diagnostics like whole genome sequencing and epigenetic testing. TMRW has also announced a partnership with TruDiagnostic to offer its clients advanced biological age testing. More at:  #Longevity #HealthTech #Funding #Startup #VentureCapital

results.title: Longevity play TMRW raises $7m seed round from Tidal and inks TruDiagnostic deal — Capital Brief
results.content:
Tmrw CEO Mark Britt. Supplied.

Your cells have an age, and it is not the same as your birthday. Working that out has become a booming industry.

While blood diagnostics have become the bread and butter of a thousand new health platforms, most will hand you a PDF with 30 data points and call it done. TMRW CEO Mark Britt is building on 1,700.

TMRW has raised approximately $7 million in seed funding to expand its clinical footprint and refine the patient experience, moving beyond digital-only health tools to a proactive clinical environment.

The round was led by Sydney-based Tidal Ventures, with participation from Melbourne fund Capital Zed and a cohort of high-net-worth entrepreneurs and family offices — the majority of whom are active TMRW members themselves. results.title: Blog | Seed Venture Capital | Tidal Ventures | Australia | New York
results.content:
### The Seed phase and beyond

The funding round will primarily support:

 Expanding the technical team to enhance the platform's AI capabilities
 Building out sales and customer success functions to support rapid growth
 Accelerating development of their regulatory trust platform features
 Supporting international expansion plans

The founders have a clear vision for growth beyond their initial market, with plans to expand their platform's capabilities and geographical reach. With their deep technical expertise in AI, strong early traction, and clear product differentiation, we believe they're well-positioned to build a category-defining company in the care sector.  
  
If you’re a visionary founder ready to make waves, please reach out via our website.



Investment Notes [...] We're thrilled to have led Minikai's Seed round, partnering with the team to revolutionise how care providers operate in highly regulated sectors through AI-powered automation. As your AI ally in disability and aged care, their platform transforms the workflow of frontline care providers by automating administrative work and compliance reporting, enabling them to focus more time on patient care while building a powerful trust platform between regulators and providers.

### Markets with tailwinds

The aged care and disability sectors present a massive opportunity, with $50bn in government funding in Australia alone. These sectors face mounting challenges that create perfect conditions for technological disruption: [...] We’re thrilled to have led MediScan AI’s Seed round, partnering with the team to transform how independent medical evaluators do their work. Today, these experts rely heavily on outsourced admin services to collate records, manage paperwork, and structure reports. Many give up 25-30% of their revenue just to get the job done. These services are expensive, outdated, and inefficient. MediScan AI is building the software that replaces them. The product helps evaluators handle more cases, in less time, with no loss of quality.

### Markets with tailwinds

results.content:

Read more.

## TMRW: $7 million

TMRW CEO Mark Britt.

Longevity platform TMRW has raised $7 million in seed funding as it looks to expand its clinical footprint and further develop its preventative health platform.

As first reported by Capital Brief, the round was led by Tidal Ventures, with participation from Capital Zed and a group of high-net-worth investors and family offices. [...] Founded by former Iflix CEO Mark Britt, TMRW says it combines advanced biomarker testing with clinical care designed to help patients track and manage their biological ageing. The platform analyses more than 1,700 biomarkers to build personalised health profiles, which clinicians use to identify risks and tailor treatment plans.

Alongside the funding, TMRW has also announced an exclusive Australian partnership with US-based epigenetic testing company TruDiagnostic, which specialises in biological age and DNA methylation testing.

## Scopey Onsite: $851,000

Scopey onsite [...] ## Scopey Onsite: $851,000

Scopey onsite 

Construction tech startup Scopey Onsite has raised $851,000 (€523,000) in pre-seed funding to accelerate development of its AI-powered platform designed to help construction teams capture and manage project data from building sites.

The round was led by UK-based SFC Capital, with additional support from Enterprise Ireland. The company was founded in Australia in 2022 and now operates across Ireland and the UK as well.

| Jan 1, 2025 | BPR Hub | $3.0M Seed | — | Accel, Global Founders Capital | [...] ## Deep Dive

### High-Level Overview

Tidal Ventures is an Australian early-stage venture capital firm focused on partnering with product-led founders primarily in Australia and New Zealand who have ambitions to scale globally. Their mission centers on helping founders build momentum as bold, courageous companies with game-changing products, especially in technology-driven sectors such as cloud services, AI, and digital platforms. They provide not only capital but also hands-on operational support, strategic advice, and mentorship to help startups navigate critical early stages and scale internationally. Tidal Ventures invests from pre-seed through Series B rounds, emphasizing a founder-first, product-first approach to unlock full potential and mitigate risks.

### Origin Story [...] Tidal Ventures serves ambitious technology founders and their nascent companies in Australia and New Zealand, providing capital and strategic partnership at the seed stage. The firm's long-term vision centers on fostering a robust ecosystem of globally competitive tech companies from the ANZ region, enabling product-led startups to scale internationally and achieve significant market impact.

results.title: Women's Health Needs Capital and Action | Bonnie Feldman, DDS, MBA posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
Melissa Bowley didn’t need a whitepaper to see the flaw. It showed up in real life, in that stretch after birth when support thins out and the system quietly steps back. The kind of blind spot you only understand when you’re in it, not studying it.
So Melissa Bowley, CEO, built Flourish Care, and Boston just watched it pull in $5.7M in seed funding. Oversubscribed, which is investor-speak for “we probably should’ve written a bigger check.” Zeal Capital Partners led the round, with Create Health Ventures, Collide Capital, Rogue Women's Fund, Symphonic Capital, Slater Technology Fund, Catalytic Impact Foundation, and Capita3 all leaning in. That’s not a cap table, that’s a chorus. [...] @ilana\_Jacqueline / IlanaWrites@gmail.com
